purpose: Which design skills the Designer Agent gets as tools, and which we drop. Keep this list tight — the agent makes better choices with 6 sharp tools than 18 fuzzy ones. Revisit after 20 agent runs based on actual usage data.

core.note: Always loaded. These are the skills the agent calls in every run. Headless-ify first.

skills.role: Produces the design brief (audience, use cases, voice) at the START of every run. Without this the agent designs blind.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: Always, exactly once, immediately after reading silver data.
skills.headless_work: Convert from interactive interview to: takes silver + preset + library as JSON, returns a structured brief JSON. No user questions.

skills.role: The scoring function. Takes a rendered page, returns rubric scores + gripes. This IS the agent's feedback loop.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: After every meaningful change, minimum every 2 actions.
skills.headless_work: Accept screenshot + HTML + rubric.json, return scores in scoring_output_schema format. Replace Nielsen's-10 with our 9 dimensions.

skills.role: Picks and tunes the typographic system — pairing, scale, measure, weight contrast.
skills.headless_work: Accept design DNA + palette, edit tailwind.config fonts + sizing, return diff.

skills.role: Tunes the palette — 60/30/10 distribution, accent usage, contrast fixes.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: When color_contrast < 7 OR distinctiveness < 7 and palette is flat.
skills.headless_work: Accept current palette + design DNA, edit tailwind colors, return diff.

skills.role: Spatial rhythm — section padding, widths, grid choice. Fixes 'every section py-16' monotony.
skills.headless_work: Accept index.astro + DNA, edit section padding/widths, return diff.

skills.role: Final pass — alignment, hover states, edge cases, mobile.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: Once craft ≥ 6 AND other dims ≥ 7, to push ship-ready to exceptional.
skills.headless_work: Accept project dir, emit a list of concrete fixes + apply them.

situational.note: Load only when the agent or rubric indicates they're needed. Not first-tier but retain.

skills.role: Amplify when the design scores safe/bland. Pushes distinctiveness up at craft's expense.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: When distinctiveness < 6 AND palette has been tuned already.
skills.caveat: Risk of over-designing for local-practice audiences. Cap max invocations per run at 1.

skills.role: Inverse of bolder — tones down. Rarely needed for dental sites (they start safe), useful when cross-pollination from poster-bold inspo pushes too far.
skills.when_agent_calls_it: When the agent just ran 'bolder' and craft or trust_signals dropped.

skills.role: Copy sharpening. Headlines, CTAs, microcopy. Fights generic-AI-copy tell.
Edge cases, error states, a11y. Pure trust + craft work.

rarely_used.note: Keep the SKILL.md files for occasional human use, but DO NOT expose to the Designer Agent. Noise > signal for this use case.

skills.reason: Dental sites rarely need motion-forward design; polish handles the 1-2 beats we want.
Easter eggs / micro-delights don't serve conversion on a local practice site.
Responsive is handled inline by our template; dedicated pass is redundant.
Astro + Tailwind static build is already performant. Revisit only if Lighthouse < 90.
Explicitly anti-goal — would destroy trust_signals for this vertical.
We already have ai-audit.js in the pipeline. Keep the skill for human use; don't double-expose.

meta.note: Not called by the agent directly — they're used BY the system.

skills.reason: Provides the vocabulary our rubric is built on. Skills reference it; agent does not invoke it.
Library ingestion. Called by our pipeline's distill-design.js, not by the agent at runtime.
Component extraction. Useful offline to grow the component library, not during a design run.

headless_work_summary.estimated_effort: 2–3 days for the 6 core; +1 day for the 4 situational; total ~1 week.
